summary: Credential and crypto-wallet stealer exfiltrating to hardcoded C2 on install
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js automatically harvests installer-side secrets and transmits them to a hardcoded attacker endpoint at http://149.28.127.35:8888 over plaintext HTTP. The script reads ~/.npmrc (npm auth tokens), ~/.env (API keys, cloud credentials, EVM private keys, DB URLs), and ~/.git-credentials, extracting token-shaped strings via regex (npm_*, registry authTokens, 0x+64-hex EVM keys). It then enumerates 71 known crypto-wallet browser-extension IDs (MetaMask `nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn`, etc.) across Chrome/Brave/Edge/Chromium/Firefox profile directories, parses each extension's LevelDB.log files for vault/mnemonic/privateKey/seed/password/keyring data, and recursively walks ~/Documents, ~/Desktop, ~/Downloads, ~/OneDrive, ~/Dropbox, ~/Google Drive and backup/keys/wallet/crypto folders searching for BIP39 seed phrases, EVM private keys, and keystore JSON. Collected data is bundled with hostname/username and POSTed to the bare-IP C2. The package advertises itself generically (keywords `lodash`, `utilities`) and index.js is explicitly labeled a dummy module in a source comment stating 'The real payload is in postinstall.js' — a cover story for the exfiltration payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
